provenance: User request: minimal conformal non-abelian model for NANOGrav FOPT

papers.title: A first order dark SU(2)_D phase transition with vector dark matter in the light of NANOGrav 12.5 yr data
papers.brief_description: Studies dark SU(2)_D gauge extension of SM with strong FOPT below EW scale. Imposes classical conformal invariance on scalar potential involving only complex scalar doublet with negligible SM Higgs couplings. FOPT at sub-GeV temperatures produces stochastic GW in nano-Hz range matching NANOGrav. SU(2)_D vector bosons serve as vector DM with non-thermal relic abundance from SM bath via scalar portal mixing.
papers.model_studied: SU(2)_D dark gauge group + complex scalar doublet, classically conformal potential, Coleman-Weinberg mechanism, negligible SM portal couplings
papers.similarity_to_target: Exact match: same gauge group, same field content (complex scalar doublet), same symmetry principle (classical conformal invariance), same target signal (NANOGrav GW from FOPT).

experimental_constraints.description: Stochastic GW background in nano-Hz frequency range from FOPT

g_D.notes: Dark SU(2) gauge coupling - FOPT strength increases with g_D

lambda.notes: Quartic coupling fixed by Coleman-Weinberg condition beta_lambda = 3*g_D^4/(32*pi^2)

assumptions.description: Scalar potential has only quartic term at tree level
assumptions.impact: Mass scales generated radiatively via CW mechanism

assumptions.description: Scalar portal coupling to SM Higgs neglected for FOPT dynamics

assumptions.description: Dark sector in thermal equilibrium with itself
assumptions.impact: Validates use of thermal effective potential

papers.title: Conformal model for gravitational waves and dark matter: A status update
papers.brief_description: Updated analysis of FOPT in classically scale-invariant model extended with new SU(2) gauge group. Computes phase transition characteristics, constrains parameter space significantly. Predicts GW spectra testable with LISA. Provides relic DM abundance predictions. Uses RG-improved effective potential, discusses renormalisation-scale dependence.
papers.model_studied: SU(2) gauge extension with classically scale-invariant scalar sector, radiative symmetry breaking
papers.similarity_to_target: Structurally identical: same gauge group, same conformal symmetry principle, same FOPT and GW production mechanism.

experimental_constraints.name: LISA sensitivity
experimental_constraints.description: Model predictions fall within LISA observational window

experimental_constraints.name: Dark matter relic abundance
experimental_constraints.description: Consistent with observed DM density in narrow parameter region

observables.name: GW spectrum
observables.notes: Computes signal-to-noise ratio, concludes model is testable and falsifiable

observables.name: Percolation temperature
observables.notes: Improved description of percolation and reheating after FOPT

g_D.notes: RG-improved analysis shows g_D > 1 produces strongest transitions

beta/H_*.notes: Inverse duration parameter - large for supercooled transitions

assumptions.description: Classically scale-invariant model with dimensional transmutation
Includes running of couplings in analysis

assumptions.impact: Narrows viable parameter space for DM relic abundance

papers.title: Gravitational waves from conformal symmetry breaking
papers.brief_description: SU(2)cSM model: conformal extension of SM with additional scalar and gauge fields charged under hidden SU(2)_X. Exhibits very strong FOPT after large supercooling. Estimates GW spectrum amplitude and frequency within LISA observational window. Discusses pitfalls in GW production estimates, solves thermal gap equation inspired by 2PI effective action formalism.
papers.model_studied: SU(2)cSM - conformal extension of SM with hidden SU(2)_X gauge group and additional scalar
papers.similarity_to_target: Structurally similar: SU(2) gauge group with conformal symmetry breaking, strong FOPT, GW production. Differs in scalar sector details (includes additional scalar field).

experimental_constraints.name: LISA observational window
experimental_constraints.description: GW spectrum falls within LISA sensitivity range

observables.name: GW spectrum
observables.notes: Amplitude and frequency within LISA window

assumptions.description: Model is conformal extension of SM
assumptions.impact: Hidden sector with SU(2)_X gauge symmetry

assumptions.description: Thermal gap equation solved using 2PI effective action formalism
assumptions.impact: Improves perturbativity during early stages of transition

papers.title: Gravitational waves from supercooled phase transitions: dimensional transmutation meets dimensional reduction
papers.brief_description: Analyzes role of higher-order thermal corrections for supercooled FOPT in models with radiative symmetry breaking (dimensional transmutation). Applies high-temperature dimensional reduction (3D EFT) framework. Finds NLO corrections have significant effect on GW observables. Discusses challenges in EFT description for strongly supercooled transitions.
papers.model_studied: Generic models with radiative symmetry breaking and dimensional transmutation
papers.similarity_to_target: Methodologically relevant: same class of models (conformal/radiative symmetry breaking), same FOPT dynamics, same GW production mechanism. Not model-specific but provides important computational methods.

assumptions.description: High-temperature expansion valid despite significant supercooling
assumptions.impact: 3D EFT framework can be applied to nucleation rate calculations

assumptions.description: Identifies challenges in EFT description for supercooled transitions
assumptions.impact: Motivates further exploration of higher-order thermal effects

papers.title: Phase transitions and gravitational waves in a non-abelian vector dark matter scenario
papers.brief_description: SM extended by dark SU(2) gauge group. Dark gauge bosons stabilized via custodial symmetry as vector DM candidates. Analyzes FOPT dynamics, computes power spectrum of stochastic GW background. Signal detectable by LISA, DECIGO, BBO, TianQin, or Taiji.
papers.model_studied: Dark SU(2) with scalar doublet, custodial symmetry, vector DM
papers.similarity_to_target: Structurally similar: same gauge group, similar scalar content, same FOPT and GW production. Includes custodial symmetry which our model has as accidental symmetry.

experimental_constraints.name: Vector dark matter
experimental_constraints.description: Custodial symmetry stabilizes dark gauge bosons as DM

assumptions.impact: Ensures dark gauge bosons don't decay

novelty_notes: The SU2_conformal model (SU(2)_D gauge group + complex scalar doublet + classically conformal quartic-only potential + Coleman-Weinberg mechanism) has been explicitly studied in the literature. Paper 2109.11558 (Borah et al., 2021) examines the EXACT same model structure with the explicit goal of explaining NANOGrav 12.5-yr data. Paper 2210.07075 (Kierkla et al., 2023) provides an updated analysis of a conformal SU(2) model with GW predictions. The model is therefore NOT novel - it is a well-studied class of models. The specific parameter space for NANOGrav matching is constrained but viable in these papers.

g_D.description: Dark gauge coupling controlling FOPT strength

m_W.description: Mass of dark gauge bosons (2 massive, 1 massless)

conventions.field_content: Complex scalar doublet Phi, gauge bosons A^a_mu

benchmark_hints.notes: This paper provides the most directly applicable benchmarks for the SU2_conformal model. FOPT at sub-GeV temperatures (T_* ~ 10-100 MeV) with g_D ~ 0.5-2.0 and v ~ 100-10000 GeV produces GW spectra matching NANOGrav. The model is classically conformal with CW mechanism.

g_D.description: RG-improved analysis shows stronger FOPT for larger g_D

conventions.dm_abundance: Narrow parameter space consistent with observations

benchmark_hints.notes: Parameters for LISA-detectable GW. Uses RG-improved potential and dimensional reduction. Excludes supercool DM scenario. Benchmarks can be adapted for PTA frequencies by adjusting v and T_*.

constraint_summary.description: FOPT must occur at sub-GeV temperatures with appropriate strength to match nano-Hz GW signal
constraint_summary.impact: Restricts T_* to ~10-100 MeV range and requires strong FOPT (alpha ~ 0.1-1)

constraint_summary.description: Non-thermal production of vector DM from SM bath must match observed density
constraint_summary.impact: Narrows parameter space; excludes supercool DM scenario in some analyses

constraint_summary.description: Model predictions for higher vev (v > 1e4 GeV) fall within LISA sensitivity
constraint_summary.impact: Models with v > 10 TeV produce GW in LISA frequency range

theory_uncertainties.description: GW predictions depend on renormalisation scale choice
theory_uncertainties.impact: RG-improved potential reduces but doesn't eliminate scale dependence
theory_uncertainties.mitigation: Use RG-improved effective potential as in 2210.07075

theory_uncertainties.description: NLO and higher thermal corrections affect nucleation rate and GW predictions
theory_uncertainties.impact: Can shift GW spectrum by O(1) factors
theory_uncertainties.mitigation: Include NLO corrections using 3D EFT as in 2312.12413

theory_uncertainties.description: Debye screening and thermal mass resummation may be incomplete for strongly supercooled transitions
theory_uncertainties.impact: Affects thermal potential shape and barrier
theory_uncertainties.mitigation: Compare with 2PI effective action approach as in 1809.11129

theory_uncertainties.description: Nucleation rate calculations have inherent uncertainties, especially for strong supercooling
theory_uncertainties.impact: Affects predicted T_* and transition dynamics
theory_uncertainties.mitigation: Cross-validate with multiple methods (EFT, lattice, semi-analytic)

recommended_next_steps.description: Confirm that SU2_conformal model parameters map directly to Borah et al. model
Read full paper to extract specific (g_D, v) benchmark points that match NANOGrav
Identify overlapping and complementary parameter regions between the two key papers
Evaluate whether vector DM production is compatible with model assumptions (negligible portal)
Verify that extracted benchmarks are compatible with semianalytic_pipeline
